workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Working on Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery's competitive positioning. Got the support ticket -- help me prep a brief.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 15:14 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 11:31 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 14:07 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-C trap (Manager brief reuses customer draft language); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-C failure mode (Manager brief reuses customer draft language)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-C -- Manager brief reuses customer draft language
